flavor_text: A gutterforged loop that leaves green stains on the finger.
Filed from chapel scrap and sold beside cracked relics.
Its inner seam is smooth from too many anxious hands.
Dull metal, no crest, only the smell of lamp soot.
Hammer marks still bite where the smith rushed the quench.
A watchman's ring heavy enough to bruise thin knuckles.
Issued at dusk to those expected back by dawn.
It sits cold and true when the gate horns start.
Mud darkens the groove where an old sigil was shaved off.
Tempered hard enough to crack a tooth in close brawls.
Shallow runes glow like embers when blood hits rain.
Silver chased with funeral knots from a nameless house.
Carried by wall wardens who count arrows, not prayers.
An oath line circles the rim in a dead commander's hand.
At first light it shines pale as a blade edge.
Lightning pitted the crown and left it hungry for thunder.
Worn by spies who bury names before entering court.
The stone is blind, yet it always points toward ruin.
A court seal stripped from a throat in the succession wars.
Gold leaf flakes away, revealing iron black as clotted wine.
Its compass mark turns wrong whenever graves outnumber homes.
Stamped with a broken crown no kingdom now admits.
It warms on the hand when the unburied walk nearby.
Dawn catches its rim like fire on a drawn wire.
A relic fit for coronations or executions, often both.
